red_baseline.reason: T029 owns deterministic artifact, host policy, isolation, relocation, Node, and real-wire contracts; generation implementation belongs to T030.
red_baseline.summary: 10/10 cases fail only at the absent scripts/build-agent-plugins.js entrypoint assigned to T030.

validation.summary: 10/10 intentional failures share the missing generator entrypoint; no handler shortcut or pre-existing artifact is accepted.
Syntax check passes for 106 JavaScript files and whitespace validation reports no errors.

review.evidence: Tests distinguish generation from readiness, require two host-specific trees with byte-identical shared components, inspect real archives and esbuild metadata, launch the relocated bundle over official-SDK stdio, and reject old Node before artifacts.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Fixtures require one canonical Skill, local stdio runtime, and separate Codex/Claude artifacts.
Artifact metadata cannot contain state/lifecycle ownership or install-time mutation scripts.
Generator report is explicitly generation-scoped with an empty readinessClaims array.
Host manifests and archive inventories contain no state migration or package-lifecycle handlers.
Separate host inventories share runtime, Skill, compatibility, and build identity bytes.
Relocated execution forbids node_modules, global CLI, npx, and install scripts.
Node >=20 metadata and an old-Node no-artifact rejection are asserted.
Runtime and canonical Skill digests plus compatibility build identity and integrity sidecars are recomputed.
The bundled real stdio process must initialize/list/call with parseable protocol stdout.
Recursive host manifest scans prohibit policy, operation, state, recovery, and authorization fields.
Tests label isolated bundle evidence generation/isolation only, never real-host readiness.
Codex and Claude path strategies and artifact identities are asserted independently.
